title: Diacalpe guatemalensis
intro: Diacalpe guatemalensis, also known as the Guatemalan Diacalpe, is a plant species belonging to the Rutaceae family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which can help alleviate symptoms of conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Diacalpe guatemalensis include limonoids, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, colds, and flu in folk medicine.
taxonomy: Diacalpe guatemalensis is classified within the plant family Icacinaceae, which consists of flowering plants commonly found in tropical regions. The Icacinaceae family is an ancient lineage that is thought to have originated in the Old World. Diacalpe guatemalensis, along with other Icacinaceae members, is part of the order Icacinales. The classification of Diacalpe guatemalensis reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plant species. This classification is based on molecular and morphological studies of the plant's characteristics.
